sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_histogram (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value FLOAT, category VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO test_histogram (id, value, category) VALUES (1, 10.5, 'A'), (2, NULL, 'B'), (3, 20.7, 'A'), (4, 30.4, NULL), (5, 40.0, 'C');
SELECT MAX(value) * 2 FROM test_histogram WHERE category IS NOT NULL;
SELECT sys_stat_histogram(value) AS histogram FROM (SELECT value FROM test_histogram WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS subquery;
SELECT MAX(value) * NULLIF(MIN(value), 0) FROM test_histogram GROUP BY category HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
SELECT value FROM test_histogram WHERE value > (SELECT AVG(value) FROM test_histogram WHERE category = 'A') * 1.05;
CREATE TABLE temp_histogram AS SELECT sys_stat_histogram(value) * 1.0 AS histogram FROM test_histogram;
SELECT sys_stat_histogram(value) FROM (SELECT id, value * value AS squared_value FROM test_histogram WHERE category IS NOT NULL) AS temp WHERE squared_value > 100;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS count, AVG(value) * (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_histogram) AS weighted_avg FROM test_histogram GROUP BY category HAVING weighted_avg > 15;
ALTER TABLE test_histogram ADD COLUMN new_value VARCHAR(50);
UPDATE test_histogram SET new_value = 'Extreme' WHERE value = (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_histogram);
SELECT category, sys_stat_histogram(value) AS hist FROM test_histogram GROUP BY category HAVING COUNT(*) > 2 ORDER BY hist DESC;
